description: UX overhaul: always-visible tab bar, permanent right detail panel (GitKraken-style), center-stage diff viewer, and full visual polish pass.

userStories.title: Always-Visible Tab Bar
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a tab bar even with one repo open so I know I can open multiple repos.
acceptanceCriteria: Tab bar renders below the titlebar, always visible (even with 1 tab) — remove the tabs.length > 1 condition in App.tsx
Single tab shows: repo name with GitBranch icon, no close button on the last tab
A '+' button at the right end of the tab bar opens the welcome screen to add another repo
With 2+ tabs: each tab shows repo name, close (X) button, active tab visually highlighted
Tab bar height is ~32px, background var(--bg-secondary), subtle bottom border 1px solid var(--border)
When welcome screen is shown (no repos open), tab bar shows empty state or is hidden
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: TabBar.tsx already exists with drag-reorder, middle-click close, etc. The main change is: (1) always render it in App.tsx, (2) add '+' button, (3) handle single-tab case (no close button). The '+' button should call onRepoOpen or navigate to welcome screen.

userStories.title: Tab Bar Visual Polish
userStories.description: As a user, I want the tab bar to look polished with hover effects, accent indicators, and hidden close buttons.
acceptanceCriteria: Active tab has a 2px bottom border in var(--accent) color
Inactive tabs use var(--text-muted) color, brighten to var(--text-secondary) on hover
Tab close (X) buttons only appear on hover — hidden by default to reduce visual noise
The '+' button is styled as a subtle icon (Lucide Plus, 16px) with hover background
Tabs truncate long repo names with text-overflow: ellipsis, max-width ~160px per tab
Tab bar scrolls horizontally if too many tabs (overflow-x: auto, hide scrollbar with CSS)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Modify TabBar.tsx and TabBar.module.css. The existing TabBar already has drag-reorder — just needs styling refinement.

userStories.title: Permanent Right Detail Panel — Always Inline
userStories.description: As a user, I want the commit detail panel to be a permanent part of the layout, not a floating overlay.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove the overlay/drawer mode from DetailPanel entirely — delete the overlay prop and all overlay-related code
Remove the useOverlayDetailPanel / DETAIL_PANEL_BREAKPOINT logic from AppLayout
The right panel is ALWAYS rendered as an inline react-resizable-panels Panel (id='detail')
When no commit is selected: the panel shows a placeholder — centered muted GitCommit icon with 'Select a commit to view details' text
When a commit is selected: the panel shows commit info (hash, author, date, message, changed files list)
The panel is resizable via the existing Separator between center and detail panels
The detail panel does NOT contain the DiffViewer — clicking a file will open the diff in the center (next story)
Remove the DiffViewer import and rendering from DetailPanel.tsx
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Key changes: (1) AppLayout always renders the detail Panel (not conditional on selectedCommit), (2) DetailPanel shows empty state when no commit, (3) Remove overlay mode entirely, (4) Remove DiffViewer from DetailPanel (it moves to center in next story). Keep the file list clickable — it will trigger center diff in the next story.

userStories.title: Detail Panel Collapse Toggle
userStories.description: As a user, I want to collapse the detail panel to give more space to the commit graph when I don't need commit details.
acceptanceCriteria: Add detailPanelCollapsed boolean to LayoutState in useLayoutState.ts (default: false)
A toggle button in the detail panel header: ChevronRight icon when expanded (click to collapse), ChevronLeft icon when collapsed (click to expand)
When collapsed: the detail Panel has size 0 (react-resizable-panels collapsible prop), center panel takes full width
When expanded: detail Panel shows at persisted size (default 25%)
A thin 24px vertical strip on the right edge is visible when collapsed, with a ChevronLeft icon to re-expand
Collapse state persists across restarts via layout state localStorage
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Use react-resizable-panels collapsible + collapsedSize={0} on the detail Panel. The thin expand strip can be a fixed 24px div rendered when collapsed, outside the Panel group. Add toggleDetailPanelCollapse to useLayoutState.

userStories.title: Center-Stage Diff Viewer — Open Diffs in Center Panel
userStories.description: As a user, when I click a file in the commit detail panel, I want the diff to open large and readable in the center area.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking a file in the detail panel's Changed Files list sets state: { viewingDiff: true, diffFile: filePath, diffCommitHash: hash }
When viewingDiff is true, the center panel shows the DiffViewer instead of the commit graph
The diff fills the entire center panel area (full width, full height minus staging area)
A breadcrumb/back bar at the top: '← Back to Graph · commitHash / fileName' — clicking returns to graph
Pressing Escape returns from diff to commit graph
File navigation ([ and ] keys) cycles through files within the current commit's changed files
The staging area below the diff is unaffected
When viewingDiff is false (default), the center shows the commit graph as normal
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: State management: add viewingDiff/diffFile/diffCommitHash to RepoView or MainContent state. The DiffViewer component is already feature-complete — just render it in a different location. The back button sets viewingDiff=false. File navigation updates diffFile. Wire the DetailPanel file click to lift state up to RepoView via a callback prop.

userStories.title: Center Diff View Styling
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the center diff view to look professional with a clean header, good typography, and readable colors.
acceptanceCriteria: Diff header bar (~40px): file path (bold, var(--text-primary)), change type badge (green=Added, blue=Modified, red=Deleted, yellow=Renamed), inline/split toggle buttons (right-aligned), file counter '1/N', prev/next buttons
Back breadcrumb: subtle link style, var(--text-muted), hover var(--text-secondary), no heavy button styling
Line numbers column has subtle var(--bg-secondary) background
Added lines: background rgba(green, 0.1), deleted lines: background rgba(red, 0.1)
Large diffs (1000+ lines) collapsed by default with 'Large diff — click to expand' placeholder
Binary files show 'Binary file changed' with Package icon
The DiffViewer has proper padding (var(--space-md)) so content doesn't touch panel edges
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Most of this styling already exists in DiffViewer.module.css. The new parts are: the breadcrumb/back bar, the diff header with file counter, and ensuring the layout works at full center-panel width (not cramped side-panel width).

userStories.title: Detail Panel Empty State and File List Polish
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the detail panel to look polished in all states with a clean empty state and refined file list.
acceptanceCriteria: Empty state: centered GitCommit icon (48px, var(--text-muted) at 30% opacity) with 'Select a commit to view details' text below in var(--text-muted)
Panel header: 'Commit Details' title (var(--font-sm), font-weight 600), collapse toggle button on the right
File list items have: Lucide status icon (FilePlus/FileEdit/FileMinus, colored), filename (bold), directory path (muted), +insertions/-deletions counts (right-aligned, green/red)
File list items have hover background (var(--bg-hover)), pointer cursor, 8px vertical padding
Currently selected file (whose diff is open in center) has accent-colored left border (3px var(--accent))
File count summary at top of list: 'N files changed, +X -Y'
Panel scrolls independently when content overflows
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: DetailPanel.tsx modifications. The empty state replaces what was previously 'no content when selectedCommit is null'. File list is already implemented — this story polishes the visual presentation and adds the selected-file highlight.

userStories.title: Global Polish — Spacing, Typography, Interactions
userStories.description: As a user, I want the app to feel cohesive with consistent styling across all panels.
acceptanceCriteria: All section headers (Commit Graph, Staging Area, Branches, Remotes, Tags, Stashes) use consistent style: var(--font-sm), font-weight 600, text-transform uppercase, letter-spacing 0.5px, var(--text-muted)
All interactive elements have focus-visible outlines for keyboard navigation (2px solid var(--accent) with 2px offset)
Hover states use consistent var(--bg-hover) background with var(--transition-fast) transition
Commit graph row hover shows subtle background highlight
Selected commit row shows 3px left border in var(--accent) color
All borders use var(--border) — no hardcoded color values
No orphaned borders, double borders, or 1px alignment gaps between adjacent panels
Staging area file items have consistent 8px padding and hover highlight
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: This is a sweep across multiple CSS module files. Check: CommitGraph.module.css, StatusPanel.module.css, Sidebar.module.css, DetailPanel.module.css, global.css. Focus on consistency — use grep to find hardcoded color values and replace with tokens. Check border alignment between sidebar/center/detail panels.
